title: Offices of Certified Public Accountants

narrative.executive_view: CPA offices offer meaningful AI-enabled labor productivity, but not an automatic windfall. Workflow automation is rapidly spreading [S1][S2], while attest sign-off, complex tax judgment and client relationships remain human. The best acquisitions will retain value through scarce talent and advisory positioning; commoditized compliance faces fee pressure.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI can accelerate audit preparation, tie-outs, research, tax drafts, client accounting and administrative throughput. CPA.com emphasizes workflow automation with human-in-the-loop verification [S2], which supports partial—not full—replacement of professionals. The supplied 5412 mix includes tax preparation, payroll and bookkeeping siblings, so it is a disclosed proxy rather than a CPA-only wage map.
narrative.value_capture: Savings may be retained where firms sell advice and complex outcomes, but hourly or hours-anchored work invites clients to demand lower bills as preparation time falls. AICPA reports technology/AI change as the leading expected issue across firm sizes, and experienced-staff hiring remains a major issue for 11–30 professional firms [S3], supporting some retention but not a monopoly.
narrative.adoption_timing: Wolters Kluwer reports 41% AI adoption globally among tax and accounting firms in 2025, 35% daily use and 77% planning more investment [S1]. Because this is global and tool use is not effective deployment, a positive three-year estimate to 50% effective adoption is more candid than treating current experimentation as complete.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The deterministic data identifies 2,286 firms in the target EBITDA band, a real but competitive acquisition universe. CPA ownership, independence and partner-compensation normalization complicate capital structures. Active consolidators and higher-quality platforms exist, but deal activity is used only for buyability and multiples, not as evidence that AI tasks are replaceable.
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal demand is durable for attest and complex tax/advisory, where professional accountability and client judgment remain valuable; routine bookkeeping and simple compliance are pressured. AI thus changes the staffing model and mix of paid work more than it eliminates a well-positioned CPA firm. This demand conclusion is distinct from whether the operator captures efficiency.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Adoption evidence is global, while the deterministic labor share is direct but 2022-vintage and the occupational mix is 4-digit. Partner draws sit outside payroll and broker-style revenue multiples can mix owner compensation with EBITDA. Fee realization and practice-sale statistics were not reopened, so capture and multiple estimates are deliberately low-confidence ranges.

sources.title: Wolters Kluwer 2025 Future Ready Accountant report release
sources.what_it_supports: Global tax/accounting AI adoption, daily use and planned AI investment; global-scope caveat.

sources.title: CPA.com Issues 2025 AI in Accounting Report
sources.what_it_supports: AICPA/CPA.com view of AI workflow automation, human verification and accounting-firm transformation.

sources.title: AICPA 2026 CPA Firm Top Issues Survey
sources.what_it_supports: Technology/AI change and staffing issues by CPA firm size.

labor_share.method: BLS Industry Productivity (IPS) direct: labor compensation (L02) $68,045.5M / sectoral output (T30) $134,726.2M, year 2022
sources: BLS Industry Productivity and Costs flat files (ip.data.1.AllData), measures L02 and T30

occupations.title: Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, and Executive
First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $263,807 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541211); class <5: 35,096 firms, avg revenue/firm $300,013 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $46,952 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 9,158 firms, avg revenue/firm $944,405 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $147,799 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 4,257 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,000,227 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $313,036 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 2,015 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,602,856 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,033,347 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 271 firms, avg revenue/firm $37,129,554 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,810,775 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 88 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,137,349,114 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $177,995,136 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 2,286
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Task-level estimate recognizes substantial drafting, reconciliation and administration automation while retaining licensed judgment and review.
caveats: 4-digit 5412 roles include non-CPA siblings; actual CPA offices likely have more accountants and fewer clerks.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Preparation and analysis automate; skepticism, sign-off and client judgment remain.
Reconciliation and write-up automate with exception review.
Internal analysis automates while partner judgment remains.

breakdown_by_role.role: Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, and Executive

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Workflow reporting automates; leadership does not.
Research/drafting automate while advisory is human.

breakdown_by_role.role: First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Mixed IT, marketing and specialist tail.

pi_dist.rationale: Talent scarcity and advisory repositioning support retention, offset by hours-based fee benchmarking.
caveats: No reopened realization-rate series; split varies among attest, tax and CAS.

pi_moat.rationale: CPA accountability, relationships and specialized workflow create a moderate moat, not immunity from automation.
caveats: Source evidence is profession-wide, not a measured small-firm retention rate.

t50_years.rationale: 41% reported global adoption is below 50%, with implementation and verification requirements delaying effective deployment.
caveats: Global survey scope and tool use are not equivalent to NAICS-effective adoption.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Wolters Kluwer reports 41% AI adoption in its global survey.
current_adoption_pct.plausible_range: Survey estimate; not a US NAICS census

historical_analogs.value: Cloud accounting workflow adoption: automation shifts professionals toward review and advisory.
historical_analogs.rationale: Both sources describe automation plus human verification and advisory evolution.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Adoption can be faster or slower by firm size.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: Leadership succession is salient in mid-size CPA firms, but no reopened source measured owner age.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: AICPA reports next-generation leadership as the top issue for 31–100 professional firms.
caveats: Leadership concern is not a formal universal succession-plan census.

active_consolidators.rationale: Multiple active CPA platforms are estimated, solely for buyability.

buy_mult.rationale: Moderate quality professional-services buy multiple after normalizing partner economics.
caveats: No reopened transaction-comparable series; owner compensation varies.

exit_mult.rationale: Scale, systems and durable client relationships allow modest expansion.

pricing_structure.value: Mix of hourly, fixed-fee compliance and advisory engagements.
pricing_structure.rationale: Sources describe workflow automation and advisory evolution, supporting a mixed-model characterization.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Broad AI use is real but effective deployment through reviewed CPA workflows remains below the 50% boundary.

terminal_value.justification: AI augments accounting workflows, while human-in-the-loop verification, licensed judgment and advisory relationships sustain paid demand [S2].

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: Consolidator and multiple estimates are confined to B/M; they do not determine V, C, A or terminal demand.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Hours-based fee pressure is reflected in pi_dist only, not in task replaceability.

dataset_mismatch.description: Role mix is 4-digit 5412 rather than CPA-office only; partner draws are outside wage data.
sensitivity: A CPA-only mix may raise professional share and lower clerical share, altering V.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: C depends on fee retention versus deflation.

reviewer_note: All score-driving URLs reopened 2026-07-21; global adoption and 4-digit role scope are disclosed.
